title: Miconia lucianobernardii
taxonomy: Miconia lucianobernardii is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It belongs to the genus Miconia, which is a group of plants commonly known as the ocreas or the melastome family. The family Melastomataceae is a part of the order Myrtales, which includes various plant families such as the myrtle family. The order Myrtales is a part of the larger clade Eurosids II, which also includes other plant families like the rose family and the heath family. The plant's classification is further supported by its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Miconia lucianobernardii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
